methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $200M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Graphyte is a Series A climate and energy company currently valued at $200M post-money, having raised $57M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (LNZA) in the same sector and stage, including Fourth Power ($150M Series A), CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M Series B), Reverion ($310M Series B), Sage Geosystems ($400M Series B), and Pano AI ($500M Series B).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from the current $200M post-money valuation to a Series B context, implying $400M. Peer-median analysis across 7 same-sector and adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $300M, with a range from $110M to $500M. The peer set spans both Series A and Series B financings, providing relevant benchmarks for developmental positioning.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $255M (low) to $460M (high), with a median of $350M. This suggests Graphyte's current $200M valuation sits below peer medians, consistent with earlier-stage positioning within the peer cohort. Key uncertainties include stage-specific capital efficiency relative to peers, commercialization timeline, and differentiation within a crowded climate tech space where Series B valuations range widely ($300M–$500M).
